name: Source Fidelity review for Neuroendocrine And Systemic Signal Reset bundle
summary: The revision 1 staged source, study, finding, intervention, and outlook records are source-faithful. PubMed metadata and abstract text support PMID 42102814 as a Cell Metabolism S1PC/LKB1/SIRT1/eNAMPT paper with aged-mouse muscle and frailty effects plus human circulating-eNAMPT biomarker context, and the current ClinicalTrials.gov API records support NCT07216781 and NCT07285629 as recruiting single-group Minicircle gene-therapy studies with no posted results. The staged records preserve the key abstract-only, registry-only, no-results, single-group, and conflict-of-interest boundaries.

findings.claim_or_issue: NCT07216781 and NCT07285629 were checked against the current ClinicalTrials.gov API during review and still had no results section, but registry status is time-sensitive.
findings.why_it_matters: The staged public wording is source-faithful only while these records remain no-results trial-watch items rather than outcome evidence.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the no-results caveat and recheck both registries before publication if the bundle is delayed materially.

findings.claim_or_issue: The PubMed abstract supports the qualitative S1PC/eNAMPT, aged-mouse muscle, frailty-index, and human circulating-biomarker claims, but not full sample-size, dose, duration, or effect-estimate extraction.
findings.why_it_matters: A public finding should not imply more quantitative certainty than the staged source record supports.
findings.recommended_action: Preserve the existing caveat that quantitative and protocol details are not fully specified in the PubMed abstract.
